reviews.review_text: ♥♥♥♥ EA. The game no longer works after the most recent update.

Need for Speed Heat is one of the best modern games in the NFS series. The day-night cycle works surprisingly well: daytime races are focused on making money, while nighttime races are about building reputation and engaging in risky chases with the police. Palm City looks stunning, especially at night with all the neon lights and rain reflections.

The career mode is top-notch, with a well-designed economy that requires you to think about what to buy and how to spend your money. The car customization is excellent and offers plenty of freedom to create your own style. Police chases can be intense and sometimes chaotic, which adds a lot of excitement. The main downside is that the story is quite short, and the ending can feel repetitive. However, if you like arcade racing, customization, and fast-paced police chases, this game is definitely worth playing.
This is the LAST great NFS game they made thats 10/10, other then underground that still not remastered.
This game has very good graphics and optimization and a very fun to play game with friends and do a lot of nice races!

bon jeu pour 2€ uniquement (70€ plein pot ça donne de l'urticaire)
bonne sensation de vitesse, musiques très bonnes et l'histoire pue mais on ferme les yeux dessus

This game is good, but the main story is meh. 

The Pursuit system is really good but bad the same time, if you have low level car, then good luck trying escape in heat 5 but when your car is level 400+ its so easy to outrun them.
2nd, when you take 90 degree L/R turn in 250km/h, how the hell the cops are keeping up with that movement -_-.

But overall i kove this game, i hope they releases another good racing game which is just as good as this if not better. ❤

The Ending was so sudden that it left me a bit shocked and unsatisfied. But despite that, story doesn't really matter much to me in this game, altho it could've been done a lot better and more intense. Some dialogue also a bit cringy to me. Facial expression is a bit scary at time. 

All of that doesn't deter the fact that this game is so much fun to play, and that's what really matter to this game. The driving and racing mechanic is smooth for an arcade game. At first i didn't like it, since i was transitioned from a semi-realistic forza horizon 4 to fully arcade-y heat. But the more i understand and learned it, it became a blast. It's so fun to pulled of smooth turning. There are so much races to do in the game and each feels unique. It also supported with the world being so fun to explore, even tho i haven't explored the off-road area much. The graphic looks fantastic, so eyecatching and satisfying. The car detail look sleek and shiny. The physic is hilarious tho, because we can almost crash into every object, even some big trees, so the game is less punishing. The drift is a lot easier than what i've experienced in forza horizon. My favorite event is sprint race, compared to a circuit one. There are also difficulty you can choose.

The only thing that i probably didn't like much is the soundtrack selection could've been better. And also i've spoiled with all the collectibles because i have the key maps dlc, that i forgot its not part of the game, and i just forgot to turn it off or uninstall it everytime. The world feels like lack of variety. and the city part looks too similar to the other. 

I still have to try all the cars and get to the maximum reputation (level). As for now, this game is a 9/10. Feels like a sweet spot to put it even higher.
this game is fun for me and the car gameplay i think is realistic

Need for Speed Heat is an absolute masterpiece and easily the best ARCADE RACER GAME. After 60 hours of playtime, the game quality remains top-tier with its addictive day-and-night gameplay loop and deep car customization. Visually, the graphics are stunning. Palm City looks beautiful under the neon lights, and the rainy nighttime streets look incredibly realistic with gorgeous wet reflections. It perfectly captures the gritty, high-stakes atmosphere of street racing. If you love racing games, this is a must-play."For me, it’s a 10/10. Sixty hours in, and I still can't get enough of drifting through Palm City under the neon lights. Buy it, customize your dream ride, and hit the streets!"

Need for Speed Heat is honestly one of the most fun modern NFS games for me. Driving around the city at night while getting chased by cops feels intense and satisfying. The customization is also really good, and there are a lot of cool cars to build however you want. The vibe, music, and street racing atmosphere make the game really enjoyable. Definitely a great game if you just want to chill and drive fast.

My experience playing this game on PC has been very different to console so far.

Back on Xbox this was one of my favorite need for speed games as it looked good visually and performed well as it was actually optimised for console.

PC is very different. Firstly, the stock graphics at 100% resolution scale which is default, look awful, especially compared to some of the older need for speed game like 2015 or rivals. If you whack it all the way up to 150-200% the game actually starts to have some clarity and looks crisper, but you will be saying goodbye to your fps.

Next is the stutters and flickering. Performance wise this game is a mess. I'm able to get reasonable frames with this game, but frankly the stability is shocking. EA cooked up a pile of ♥♥♥♥ with this one.

Tbh I'm not going to rate this game poorly as I think it's actually a really good game that is very poorly optimized for PC and needs to be messed around with a bit for it to be a good experience, which is what I plan to do, and then I expect it will be a very good game. Let's see.

Graphics & sound design are fantastic, so I'd recommend this to anyone who likes racing games but. I just don't seem to enjoy them anymore. I used to when I was little. Need For Speed World was so fun.
For an EA game theres little if any micro transactions, and overall a well done acade style racing game
I couldn't force myself to keep playing this, the dialog is cringe as hell and its boring as hell. It has no danger like older nfs games like rivals, highstakes and hot pursuit.

One of the most terrible NFS zero immersion, zero vibes, even NFS 2015 is superior. 

- Abysmal traffic and abysmal AI although accurate of how west drives on the road. 
- Map is designed for hot wheels 
- Cops spawn out of nowhere for no apparent reason, making chases actually very unimmersive 
- Tuning is nonexistent(better go play Night Runners)  
- Unnecessary grind
- Rather bad dialogue

The game’s a blast, classic Need for Speed vibes. It’s not the best in the series, but it’s solid all around. The graphics are slick, and the cars look super detailed. Gameplay feels good, and the events are balanced. The story’s pretty light, but at least it’s not overly wordy.

There are two significant downsides, though: the cutscenes were stuttering like hell on my modern PC, no matter what I tried, borderless mode, no overlays, FPS caps, you name it. And the cops are way too powerful at night (I played on Medium difficulty), until you get a car over 350. It’s really frustrating when you get busted almost every time, and it just feels like a waste of time.

If you can get over these 2 things, I recommend to any NFS fan or simply to arcade racing lovers.

Got this at a deep discount during steam sale a while back. After starting the game my PC almost crashes from the CPU load. Seems like the game is poorly optimized and overstresses CPU at all times, even while sitting in the menu. There are solutions on the internet but none worked for me. Will try to refund but won't hold out hope. Shame.

If you want a good racing game (that doesn't destroy your PC) just get Tokyo Xtreme Racer.
what if you made a game where every car understeers and feels like ass unless you drift on every corner but the drifting also felt like ass

It's not bad but ultimately the negatives outweigh the positives. 

The bad: 
- It's an EA game so you will have to install their launcher, create and account, and then you will have to deal with the EA app launching by default on startup, etc.
- The cops are rubberbandy as hell. When the heat level goes up, no matter how fast you're going some cop will spawn behind you at a thousand miles an hour and ram you to the middle of next week. You're supposed to use ramps and gimmicks like that, good old fashioned driving won't help you. This was a dealbreaker for me.
- I didn't like the soundtrack, way too much reggeatón.

The good: Some solid NFS action, great graphics, cool cars, very good sound. The handling is very arcade, more than I expected, but it was still fun for a little bit.

i didnt even get past the first hour because the mechanics of this game are so ass. the game says im driving 150km/h when im barely moving, when i brake the car stops almost immediately, i cant even turn my damn camera, the dialogues are as annoying as they are in forza horizon and the camera field of view is atrociously small. the steering is ass because somehow when i stop turning right or left my character still does for like a solid 0.7 seconds until i go straight again. when i turn i make the shuge ass bows or very tiny itty bitty ones. the drifting is straight up ♥♥♥♥. i felt like i was in a cage with every single second that i played this game. go play forza horizon. oh and for some reason the driver is sitting in the right seat while everyone is driving on the right side of the road, is this supposed to be usa or uk? make up your minds. and i forgot to mention, SOMEHOW even tho my pc is a ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ beast my game lagged like ten times just when i opened the game and saw some cutscenes. the cutscenes look like ♥♥♥♥. this game is optimised like ♥♥♥♥. the police is ♥♥♥♥ because they somehow outrun me even tho im at full speed in a damn sports car. go play forza horizon 4 or any gta v or even ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ dying light the beast brcause the physics in these games make sense

Absolute garbage.
Can't even properly launch without cooking my cpu, can't handle a single cutscene properly, everything is 1fps. And when it finally loads and i can click a button, loading into a lobby fries my cpu again.
When i do eventually get into the game, i can run everything at max settings at 60fps no problem.
Filth and i genuinely consider this malware at this point.

This is a very fun game the campaigns pretty good and the progression is rewarding and visuals still are good but no sim manual h pattern support its easy to replay, difficulty is pretty easy but the cops are out for blood.    8.5/10
It is a good game and please play this game after you have played NFS Unbound if you do vice versa then you are going to regret.

It's what video games are made for. Not too complex, but also not boring. The story is beautifully campy and can be neglected, or not. There are a lot of customization options you definitely have to explore. Plus, great community libraries. Just enjoy the ride.

A great game for people who like to race under pressure, full of adrenaline and a game with a great potential for enjoyable grinding, I recommend the base game, the deluxe edition seems to not be worth the money.

This was my first Need for Speed game and my first proper racing game on PC and I absolutely loved it!

When I asked my friends for recommendations, they suggested games like Assetto Corsa, which focus on realistic racing. While I can appreciate that style, I grew up enjoying arcade racers like Asphalt on mobile, so NFS Heat was right up my alley. I love the lawlessness of it all the high-speed chases, smashing through traffic, and trying to escape relentless cops. The chaos is what makes the game so much fun.

The game runs great on my PC overall, but I do have a couple of minor gripes.

The first issue is with certain cinematic cutscenes. Oddly enough, only the pre-rendered cinematics stutter, while in-game cutscenes run perfectly. For example, there was a scene where the protagonists were watching the news on a TV. The main cutscene played smoothly, but the news footage shown on the TV would stutter and occasionally miss audio cues. It wasn't game breaking, but it was distracting enough to hurt immersion. This could be an issue specific to my Graphic card, but hopefully EA addresses it.

My second gripe is the car balance. It's a bit funny that some of the fastest cars in the game are older models. The Porsche 911 RSR and the DLC McLaren F1 are so dominant that once you unlock them, there's very little reason to use most other cars. The RSR in particular is incredibly versatile and excels in almost every type of race except drifting. This hurts variety because it encourages players to rely on the same handful of vehicles. It's a shame, considering the game features such an amazing selection of cars that end up being overshadowed by these two.

Overall, this is a fantastic racing game. If you're looking for an action-packed arcade racer where you're constantly being chased by cops, weaving through traffic, and competing against other racers, this game delivers exactly that. I had an absolute blast and would happily recommend it.

One fair warning, though: the cops are relentless and at higher heat levels, genuinely terrifying. Drive carefully, and happy racing!

Things I hate:
- Daytime music is cringe.
- Characters are cringe.
- Cops are RUTHLESS.
Other than that, the game, for the most part, is pretty good. Night time atmosphere is pretty cool. Many cars & customizations to spend money on. Gameplay is fun.

Controller setup is atrocious. I own a good Hall Effect controller (I can't configure it, thanks EA) and I'm not about to buy another one just for this game. I absolutely hate that EA forces its bloated launcher on you even when playing through Steam. Extremely frustrating and unnecessary. Come on, EA — just let us play the damn game.

I'll never buy another EA game.

The heat in the title actually comes from your blood pressure going up while playing. I’d rank this below NFS 2015 and Unbound because, for me, it just didn't check the vibe box. The irony is that the handling model is actually better here when compared to those two games.

The day/night cycle was a great concept, and the cop chases can be interesting, but your car takes way too much damage, making the chases more annoying than challenging. As always, the game looks good, but I just didn't like the setting. 

In NFS 2015, Heat, and Unbound, the open world feels soulless (especially in Heat) acting as little more than a pretty menu screen where you just teleport to races or follow hand-holding GPS lines through maps cluttered with lazy checklist icons. While nostalgia definitely plays a role, the proof is in the design: I still remember most of Underground 2's tracks by heart (and maybe a few from 2015 due to the prestige races), but the newer games are completely forgettable.

Underground 2 forced you to actually live in the city, where cruising down the highway and spotting a faint neon light down a hidden alley meant you discovered a secret shop that actively unlocked parts, giving you a real reason to memorize every shortcut and corner. It treated the map like an actual playground to explore, whereas the newer titles treat the open world like a chore you have to drive through just to get to the next loading screen.

I know the modern games have significantly larger maps, but scale does not equal substance. Even after almost 200 hours in Unbound, I still constantly need the mini-map to navigate because the environment is just so generic and repetitive. While there might be some visual cues scattered around, the game just fails to make me care enough to learn them. When a game handles all the navigation for you and treats its own world like a hollow backdrop, it breeds player apathy.

On every technical metric, the newer games are superior, but Underground 2 keeps its legendary status because it had a soul and was sitting right in the middle of car culture at that time. Will the newer games be rembered the same?! I’m giving this a thumbs up strictly because I’m still running on hopium for a proper Underground 3, and I refuse to believe this was it.
Get it on a deep discount and try it!

The Ultimate Modern Street Racing Experience!
After putting 49 hours into this game, I can confidently say this is one of the best Need for Speed entries in years.

Genius Day & Night Concept: Racing legally during the day to earn cash (Bank), then switching to thrilling illegal street races at night for reputation (Rep) creates a perfect gameplay loop.

Intense Cop Chases: The police in this game are relentlessly aggressive, especially during high-Heat nights. Escaping them is nerve-wracking but incredibly satisfying.

Superb Car Customization: The visual and performance tuning is top-notch. You can customize everything from body kits to the literal exhaust sound of your car.

A rock-solid arcade racer with gorgeous neon-drenched visuals. Highly recommended! 9/10.

On top of the godawful and hyper unrealistic plot, the voice acting is cringeworthy and the physics not great.  Perhaps the worst problem with this game, however, is the grind.  You will not be able to afford the higher end cars until after you beat the entire game, and even then expect to spend tens of hours simply re-running old races just to slowly gain enough money.... all just to buy a car that you can't use since there isn't anything left to do.  

The scaling of income needs to be exponential.  Later races need to pay 100-200k instead of 30k, otherwise people are just going to cheat.
cool gameplay, good graphics & good camera feature, but for modification / body kit, i prefer unbound
this game has very good gameplay, there's many customization and cars, the story also good, very recommended if you like highly customizable cars in-game
The cutscenes glitch on my Lenovo Legion 15IRX10, but the gameplay is super smooth and no crashes so, it's not a hardware issue. Someone's gotta fix that.

I put this game off for a while thinking it was be pretty average (also because it wouldn't work all the way with my racing setup) but it made me fall in love with the NFS franchise again. I forgot how fun these games were.
Amazing graphics, nice car options and car designs. Story was a little short but this game was good

Probably the best modern era need for speed, read this review if ur thinking about playing this game(wait for sale on it) as i have planty of experience with nfs games and im not casual brainrot zoomer.

Need for speed games ive played before this one - underground 1 and 2, most wanted, carbon, pro street, undercover, the run, hot pursuit remaster, most wanted 2012, shift for mobile, and no limits mobile game.

Heres quick review covering all basic/important features of the game:

Heat has great graphics and visuals and is decently optimised, i personally play it on 1080p 144hz monitor on ultra settings + 200% resolution scaling with lossless scaling on (no latency on 3090 and its not like it matters cuz game itself has steering delay).

There are a lot of cars and you wont have problem gettin em cuz there are lot of ways to earn money, races dont loose much on the rewards once you complete them and there are many of them so it wont happen like in old nfs games where you simply had no good ways of earning money once you finish story and all races, here u can replay them and they come with different performance ratings and still retaining decent cash payout therefore allowing you to easly grind up the money for any vehicle or upgrade u want.

Game has beautiful map, weather and day/night switch mechanic makes it even better,
my problem with the map tho is that it has a lot of pointless open space left for people who would for some reason want to off road in a need for speed game, and thats why its there, cuz they wanted to bring forza players in, basically they tried to pull of similar thing they later tried with battlefield 2042.
There are lots of collectables over the map too making free roaming bit more fun, at first i intended to collect em all and 100% the game but i gave up cuz handling/driving in this game is just that awful and annoying.

Story and its characters are forgettable and mid at best, character clothing and stuff like that are pointless and selfie zoomer animations/style after races and in garage are cringe and annoying but i guess thats what car scene today is like sadly...(it got much more worse in nfs unbound)

Cops are pretty good and fun, at night stakes get higher the more u progress trough the night cuz u risk loosing everything u earned for that night if u get busted, car health and pretty decent cop ai and heat system make for some challenging and thrilling experience altho it gets bit easier when u get to faster cars. in general they feel like step up from old nfs games, altho much different experience from mw cops they still make for very good gameply and are in no way inferior to og ones.

Handling is horrible, it feels like they were done by mobile game devs as game forces you to drive only in once certan way meaning that you cant grip around corners but have to break to drift everywhere you turn, drifting is bad too as you dont have full control of your car not in drift nor in grip and intentional delay on controls makes it hard to make split second decisions and adjust for in last second-  you dont have full control of your vehicle, i hate driving in this game to its core and sense of speed is pretty mid too.
Opponent AI is bad too, being rarely challenging on hard i often found myself underpowering my car to have lower rating so i coud enjoy it more, also for whatever reason when playing on hard difficulty opposing ai racer who is first will always gap everyone by 1km on average as if his ai thinks that everyone else has laped him so he drives in rubberband mode to catch up lol.

Music is ass and i played with it off from the start, instead ive made playlist that includes all the good need for speed songs in the franchise.

Im bit lazy to write more about it so ive covered just basics of the game you have youtube for more extensive ones, over all its a decent street racer and i do recommend it if u want more need for speed or some casual brainrotty racing game, dont buy it if u want good driving experince especially if u intend to play with wheel lol.

almost 8 out of 10 .
Need for Speed Heat is the greatest racing game of all time. So many hours of fun content, whether you’re a car junkie or not! Highly recommend!
Hands down best modern NFS experience for me, I love everything about this game except couple of soundtracks which I am glad I can cycle, could have added more DLC but oh well that is EA for you.

Another one of those games that gets a tentative thumbs up. 

Racing-wise it's decent fun, cop chases are lacking though. The older games had elements on the map to help you take out cops, in this game there isn't anything really except jumps over water where the cops tend to fall in, and those are in only a few places on the entire map. So in the end it feels undercooked. While you have a starter car you will find it extremely difficult to outrun the police due to the lack of these kind of mechanics, and it's just frustrating and tedious.

From a nostalgia perspective, it gets another tentative thumbs up, because while iconic cars from NFS' past are present they dont sound the same, so it really dampens the experience.. but they look the same, so..

Customisation wise, for paintjobs/decals/vinyls it's essentially top-notch, or at least as good as I remember Pro-Street being. Body parts though, definitely a step down from Pro-Street/Carbon where you could fine tune everything. Some cars have very little options. Performance customisation is fine, have nothing to say about it.

Solid 6/10, worth the cheap price I paid.
Get it guy , It got good graphics. You can chill , race or cruise or even hiigh andreline rushes. Worth it in sales etc.
this game sucks on cut scens dont buy it worst experience on my rtx 50060
Great nfs heat game, runs good, sounds good, feels good. DLC's are fun to do if you 100% the game and just wanna do some more side quests!
best car game ive ever played so far n cheap, worth the try

NFS Heat is a fun and visually impressive arcade racer with a satisfying day/night cycle, balanced difficulty, smooth performance, and progression that feels worth every penny even at full price. The map looks stunning and the car customization keeps you engaged for hours. That said, the lack of ray tracing is a real letdown for a game this visually ambitious. While street lights do reflect nicely on car bodies, there are zero reflections of your car on the ground and no light occlusion behind the vehicle, making it feel like your car is floating rather than actually being part of the world. It's not a dealbreaker, but for a game that clearly cares about visuals, it's a glaring omission that has never been patched. Overall, nothing groundbreaking, but a solid racer that delivers where it counts.

I had way more fun with Need for Speed Heat than I expected. The day/night system is awesome: chill racing during the day and intense police chases at night keep the game exciting. Customization and visual effects are great, the cars feel fun to drive, and the neon city vibe looks amazing. Especially liked the amount of extended pre-made wraps.

The story is pretty forgettable and some races get repetitive, but overall it’s one of the better modern NFS games for sure.

8/10.

I've been looking for a racing game to kill some time w and nfs is a cult classic, Insane visuals, freedom to custom wrap your car and looks, a great collection of cars, and adrenaline packed cop chases. The mechanics are very barebones, you've got basic clutchless manual shifts, handbrakes and drifting, making it an arcade racer more than a proper driving experience. If you're looking to kill some time with very forgiving mechanics, this is your game.
The downside includes it being an EA game which should speak for itself, and also EAs attempt to piss pf traditional pc players (TAB to pause, Q E to select tabs i mean its lowk ragebait) but yeah overall good game, i bought it on sale for 90% off so im happy w it

Need for Speed Heat is one of the better modern NFS games because it finally understands the formula: stylish cars, night racing, police pressure, customization, and speed that feels aggressive. It is not perfect, but it has attitude. The day/night system gives the game a strong rhythm, and the car culture vibe actually works.

Verdict: Recommended.

Need for Speed Heat delivers one of the best modern arcade racing experiences. The day-and-night system keeps gameplay fresh — legal races during the day for cash, illegal street racing at night for REP and intense police chases. The car customization is deep, handling feels responsive, and the neon-lit city atmosphere gives strong classic NFS vibes. Story is short, but the gameplay loop and progression make it addictive for racing fans. A solid comeback for the franchise.

The game is very grindy but it's worth it in the long run. The cars look nice and sound nice too, the map is pretty good too. Collectables and activities are a nightmare but that 100% completed makes it worth it. Online is still very active, I was playing with people that needed help with the achivements that I met from NFS Heat discord severs.
So overrall I give the game a 8/10 would've gave it a 9 if the collectables weren't so brain numbing to get

A fun N4S game, it's not something you will really remember or go back to but it's decent
8.5/10 I didn't like the police mechanics, but it is a fun and good racing game.

best and most fun racing game ever. the cop system is just perfect.

I really want to love this game but the campaign story is too short. Lot's of things you can do with Torres. Im recommending this game because i got it on a discount, great car costumization, amazing car handling for this racing genre, killer soundtrack (needs more house/disco genre), amazing map, great graphics style, and a Polestar 1.

best nfs modern game good customizations and good handling i like that engine swap feature. i would say if EA continue to support online mode then this game will live forever. btw yes i have very low en pc and i play in 20 to 30 fps but it is still fun.

There a something you must address first,there a probability that it will very laggy on most of the maps not because of your spec is bad but the game has TERRRIBLE optimization for pc. it will absolutly ruin your experience since it's a racing game.

if this ever happen to you just type "NFS heat fps fix script" on google and you will figure out the rest,

For the game itself i just has very personal issue with the story because it's TOO SHORT, there lot potential here and there that will make it satifying to enjoy but it cut short with lot of conlict that just solve itself or easily without any real struggle and characters that need more screen time to be memorable.

Other that that i can say this game very decent,quite amount cars and customization,freedom to decorate your car,good sense of speed,cars that felt have weight and races that have lot of type like drag,dirt,and drift. and with mechanic of  day(money) and night (reputation) they both become essential to each other since you need money to buy parts and reputation to make it available to buy.because of that police chase has reason since it will multiply your reputation depending your heat level.

For starters, wait for a sale. I'd never ever pay full price for this game, I wouldn't have done so even on the very day it released. 

I got this game because I felt like reliving some nostalgia from the original Most Wanted, and honestly it did achieve that to an extent. I got super immersed blasting my own playlists and grinding night races. If that's what you're looking for you have to try this game. The car selection and customisation potential is really quite impressive. 

Everything else falls short. For most of the game the cops feel very overtuned. They will stick to your car like glue no matter what you do and they'll probably kill you and ruin a whole session of grinding. The civilian AI is annoying, they like to stop in intersections for some reason. The story is pretty meh and really short, you could finish the game in 10 hours easily. I happen to have a lot more hours because I found the game fun to play with a friend and I decided to get all the collectibles. 

Ultimately there is a decent amount of substance to this game and I would recommend it to any racing fan (on sale). It is not nearly as good as it could have been, but it's still something.

In short: decent 6/10 racing game. Worth buying only on sale.

I really like the idea of earning money legally during the day and reputation illegally at night, while risking your money to the police. However there are dozens of small things that make the experience a lot worse, so lets talk about them.

For context: I have beaten the whole game and all the achievements on hard difficulty, and while I have not completed many racing games, I still think that I have enough of them completed to have a strong opinion on this genre. And I know that this game is a long forgotten project of EA, and it will never receive updates, but the point of this review is just to give you some ideas on how a racing game can be better, so you could choose better ones to spend your time with in the future.

[h2]Things that I liked[/h2]
[list]
[*]Day/night system, especially speed traps increasing your heat at night — it blends into the game so well.
[*]Game is very balanced. On hard difficulty every challenge feels just right. 3 repairs at night makes you think twice and drive more carefully, as this can directly lead to being busted on high heat. Some races are really hard, but Top 3 is enough to win a rare part or money/rep, which makes sense since the main focus is escaping police rather than winning races.
[*]Controls. Every car feels easy to control according to how you tuned it, which means your car behaves as you would expect.
[*]Map. Not the best one NFS games have to offer, but after playing NFS Payback I am grateful for this kind of variety. I especially like Downtown Palm City where you need to jump through the river occasionally.
[/list]

[h2]What I didn't like[/h2]

[h3]Bugs[/h3]
This game holds up really well bug-wise, but when they exist they are annoying. From what I have noticed:
[list]
[*]Waypoints sometimes do not show the route depending on where you placed them.
[*]A lot of things stop working while animations play. For example after completing a drift zone, you cannot enter the dealership until the unlock animation disappears, with no way to skip it. I don't see a real reason why I can't skip something I don't care about.
[*]Sometimes long jumps register more or less meters than you actually travelled.
[*]Sometimes police just lose sight of you for no real reason. While this is probably intended on lower difficulties, I don't think it should be a thing on hard difficulty.
[/list]

[h3]Activities[/h3]
For other activities the heat system seems stupid to me, because why would the police have some way to measure your long jumps and drifting abilities in the first place..? And why would you even do drift zones to increase your heat at night, if you need a drift-tuned car for this, which is very unoptimal for escaping cops. Also the rewards from completing activities are just not worth it. Lets be real, no one cares about some pink underglow or other customization stuff, or if they do, they will just complete certain activities for their desired customization part and ignore the rest. A fix would be to remove heat increasing from drift zones and long jumps entirely, and instead tie them to their own subplots. For example...
[list]
[*]A drift king placing zones to find worthy competitors so you would unlock drift plot after getting certain amount of stars from drift zones. 
[*]A crazy old stuntman placing long jumps to find promising young stuntman to teach. You get noticed after earning enough stars on long jumps.
[/list]

[h3]Collectables[/h3]
Just why flamingos? I don't get the reasoning behind this. Basically we have 3 types of collectables: street art, flamingos and billboards. The problem? None of them fit in the game. All of them unlock the same lame customization parts as activities do, so the real reason to collect them is only if you want to get all achievements, which for me is just an annoying, long and unfun process. I don't really see the point of any collectable in this game.
[list]
[*]As far as I am concerned billboards are police propaganda, so that when destroyed, it could make more racers populate the city and unlock harder races.
[*]Flamingos could be replaced with advertising signs of a Lucas mechanic business competitor, where destroying them earns you parts and tuning discounts. This would also justify raising part prices, making the day/night economy actually matter.
[*]Add possibility to apply grafitti after finding it on the map. It would make hunting cool grafitti meaningful.
[/list]

[h3]Map[/h3]
Map is missing advanced filters. Yes, you can change the tabs of the map which I guess you can count as a filter, but some random website has a better map with better filters than the in-game one, which is crazy to me. Just add the ability to set multiple filters, like for example being able to see locations and activities only. Also I really missed the ability to just move the map with the mouse. I don't believe that it is that hard to implement and it would make map panning much easier.

[h3]Garage[/h3]
Through the whole game I used only one car, tuning it towards drift, offroad or race depending on the next event and I really missed presets. Just let the player save and switch tuning setups without visiting the garage between every race. Part prices are also way too low. I never bought a new car through the whole plot, and always had enough money for the best parts without ever needing to do day races, which kills the whole purpose of the day/night idea. Raising prices and adding presets would fix both issues at once.

[h3]Dealership[/h3]
No filters, and hard navigation through cars nobody cares about. By the way there are way too many cars in this game that almost no one uses, and while it is a good idea to give people the opportunity to drive their dream car, I don't believe that anyone wants to drive an ACURA RSX-S '04 (sorry for anyone who does). I just see this as unnecessary clutter, when you have legendary cars and hypercars like the Nissan Skyline or Koenigsegg Regera. Just add sorting filters and cut down the number of cars.

[h3]Music[/h3]
There is just not enough music. After 10 hours of playing this game you already hate every single song as you have listened to them too much. And if you especially hate one of them, why can't you just remove it from playing in settings or at least skip forward to the next song? Removing a certain song from playing at all in settings was implemented in NFSU2 which was more than a dozen years ago, and you can't implement this in a newer game? Multiple radio channels with different vibes like rock, pop or chill would go a long way here.

[h3]Settings[/h3]
Why isn't there a way to change controls? Such a basic thing that almost every game in the world has and this one does not. And I would never believe that this is something hard to code, more like the devs were lazy to ever add it. Just add the possibility to remap buttons and remove certain songs from the playlist.

[h3]Plot[/h3]
Too short, and new characters pop up out of nowhere for no reason. It feels like it was rushed, which really makes the experience worse. Also, why is the whole plot around the police taking care of the racers they bust, but they never take the player's car? Either change the plot, or make the police "steal" the car from busted players according to the plot. Extending it at least twice and adding subplots like the stuntman one I mentioned earlier would make a big difference, and please give the opportunity to skip cutscenes if the player wants to!

[h3]Achievements[/h3]
As with other NFS games published on Steam, this has online achievements as well, which is strange to me, as everyone knows that almost nobody will play this game after ~5 years from the publish date, which would make getting those achievements much harder. Remove the online ones, and add ~30 new achievements that the player will mostly get while progressing through the plot.

P.S: I probably should just create my own game at this point.

After years of skipping through the Need for Speed series since the NFS Rivals era, a game that was fun but personally felt lacking due to its limited visual customization, [b]Need for Speed Heat finally feels like the franchise returning to its glory days.[/b]

[h3]Graphics[/h3]
The graphics powered by Frostbite 3 are undeniably impressive. Even on low settings, this game still looks stunning. Palm City feels alive with its signature neon aesthetic, blending iconic 80s-inspired visuals with a modern atmosphere. The late 2010s pop culture influence is also strongly present through its flamingo icons, graffiti, and overall city identity. Even though I only started playing this game in 2026, Heat genuinely gave me flashbacks to the late 2010s. [b]Visually and atmospherically, Palm City is absolutely mesmerizing.[/b]

[h3]Map & Open World[/h3]
The map is another major highlight. Palm City is incredibly vast, but more importantly, it feels meaningfully explorable. Most of the world is accessible, from roads and highways to forests, fields, swamps, and beaches. [b]This freedom makes the open world feel alive rather than artificially restricted by invisible barriers.[/b] It never becomes boring because even when you get tired of asphalt roads, the environment still encourages exploration.

[b]What makes this even better is how the game gives purpose to its massive map through Collectibles and Activities.[/b] Hunting flamingos, smashing billboards, chasing speed traps, completing drift zones, and hitting long jumps all encourage players to explore every corner of Palm City. This makes the world feel rewarding instead of oversized for no reason. This is exactly the kind of open world design I have been looking for in a Need for Speed game.

[h3]Vehicles[/h3]
[b]The vehicle variety is also impressive.[/b] From street-legal vehicles ranging from pickups to hypercars, the selection keeps gameplay fresh. As someone who loves car culture, visual customization is essential, and Heat absolutely delivers here. [b]Customization options are diverse,[/b] and while not every car has equally extensive options, that honestly reflects real automotive culture.

[h3]Balancing[/h3]
[b]Balance is another area where Heat succeeds.[/b] Your starter car does not feel disposable thanks to engine swaps and flexible upgrade paths, meaning your early game choice can still compete with high-end cars later on. Progression feels fair without excessive grinding. AI racers also feel far less frustrating compared to older rubber banding systems like Need for Speed: Most Wanted (2005), making upgrades actually feel meaningful.

[h3]New Concepts[/h3]
[b]The Day and Night system is one of Heat’s best innovations.[/b] Daytime offers a more relaxed experience focused on earning cash, while nighttime becomes intense and high-risk for reputation gains. During the day, police are manageable unless provoked. At night, however, the game becomes brutal. The police feel aggressive, organized, and genuinely threatening, even more intense than what I personally experienced in Most Wanted 2012 or Rivals. This duality creates a fantastic balance between relaxed gameplay and high-adrenaline chaos.

[h3]Disappointment[/h3]
[b]My biggest disappointment with Need for Speed Heat is how short-lived its post-launch support was.[/b] The foundation was incredibly strong. Gameplay, map design, customization, and atmosphere all had massive long-term potential, but it feels like the game’s future was cut short far too early. Its live service lifespan ended much sooner than it deserved, especially after Criterion shifted focus toward Battlefield. As a result, Heat feels like a game that successfully returned Need for Speed to greatness, but never got the chance to reach its full potential. With such a solid foundation, Heat could have evolved much further and possibly become one of the greatest peaks in Need for Speed history.

[h3]Final Thoughts[/h3]
Thank you, Ghost Games, for creating a title that truly brought back the spirit of Need for Speed while refreshing it with modern ideas, strong street culture, and one of the best open worlds the franchise has ever had.

This game is not good. Here's why. In other NFS escaping the police is in your hands. If you thread a closing needle the cops can't make, you loose them or at least gain distance. Do the same thing with this game and a cop well be right behind you as if it spawned there. At some point  they stop chasing for no apparent reason. Sometimes you're just busted for no apparent reason. No police touching you. I wander what they thought the point of the game was.
